disambiguation: This incident is not a benign autoscaler or CI burst because the principal's high novelty score and incomplete tag completeness indicate potential misuse, contrasting with expected behavior from a legitimate autoscaler which would typically have consistent tagging and lower novelty.
key_evidence: 22 instances launched in a short time frame (124 seconds) by the same principal (service:autoscaling.amazonaws.com).
The principal has a high novelty score (344) indicating unusual behavior.
The tag completeness is 0.8 for some events, suggesting incomplete ownership or tagging of resources.
The events occurred during regular business hours (not off-hours).
likely_intent: Malicious resource hijacking through unauthorized instance launches.
recommended_guardrails: Implement strict IAM policies to limit the actions of the autoscaling service.
Set resource quotas to prevent excessive instance launches.
Enable logging and monitoring for unusual patterns in instance creation.
